title: Daylight Sensor
description: Outputs a redstone signal proportional to the sky light level. Signal strength 0-15 based on time of day. Right-click to toggle between daytime mode (peak at noon) and inverted/nighttime mode (peak at midnight). Used to automate lights, doors, and farms by time of day.

steps.title: Sensor block
steps.explanation: Place a daylight sensor block at (0,1,0). It must have an unobstructed view of the sky (no blocks above it). The block emits signal strength 0-15 from all six faces. During the day the signal peaks around noon (strength 15); at night it drops to 0 unless you right-click to invert.

steps.title: Output wire and lamp
steps.explanation: Dust at (1,1,0) carries the signal east. A redstone lamp at (1,0,-1) shows whether daytime output is active. Right-click the sensor to toggle inverted mode (night-mode) — the lamp will then light only at night.
